prevalence.source: WHO scabies burden estimates; tropical dermatology surveillance
prevalence.trend: stable; outbreaks in institutional and low-income settings

incidence.population: General population; higher in crowded households

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant itch, sleep disturbance, and secondary infection risk

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Typical pruritic papules and burrows; diagnosis usually clinical; dermoscopy or microscopy can confirm mites/eggs when needed

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; outbreak- and symptom-driven detection
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands annually in endemic areas
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; requires treatment of close contacts and hygiene measures

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care; confirmatory microscopy limited to reference services
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Topical permethrin or oral ivermectin; treat household contacts; environmental cleaning of bedding/clothes
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Reinfestation due to incomplete contact treatment and crowded living conditions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective low-cost therapies available in public system
